degraded: Prior-iterate E2E spec 77 fails on this stack — its outage premise needs the WS upgrade to be REJECTED (pty spawn failing), but here the pty spawned (WS 200) so the terminal connects and there is no outage to surface. NOT a regression from this client-only change (a client wake detector cannot alter server pty spawn). Environmental, per project_e2e_pty_spawn_267_dead_cwd.

behaviors.behavior: Wake detector does NOT fire on a normal-gap tick (running/idle foreground tab)
Wake detector FIRES on a large tick gap (the tab was frozen/slept)
Wake detector re-arms — a second sleep fires again
stop() clears the interval and prevents any further wake; idempotent
On wake, an OPEN-but-stale socket is PROBED (ping) with no browser event
On wake with NO socket, reconnect fires immediately
dispose() stops the detector so a later tick does nothing
No spurious wake under the shared fake-timer seam — full 59-test WS suite green
REAL BROWSER: a lid-close simulation (page.clock.fastForward) reconnects a half-open socket with NO focus/visibility/online event
behaviors.evidence: e2e/flows/78-terminal-wake-reconnect.spec.ts — PASS; verified load-bearing by disabling the detector and rebuilding: FAILED with 'Expected: > 1, Received: 1'

behaviors.behavior: Real macOS lid-close→unlock on the reporter's Mac recovers in a few seconds
behaviors.evidence: A genuine OS suspend cannot be produced in CI or a local harness; the mechanism (clock-drift detection → reviveIfStale → probe → reconnect) is unit-covered end-to-end and demonstrated in a real Chromium via page.clock's documented lid-close simulation (ledger row above).

surface_verification.justification: Client WS-behaviour change, so jsdom cannot prove the real outcome. Executed e2e/flows/78-terminal-wake-reconnect.spec.ts in real Chromium against an isolated single-process stack (temp USERPROFILE, built client via SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR, Hono 127.0.0.1:3851): 1 passed. It drives the exact Mac mechanism — page.clock.fastForward (Playwright's documented laptop-lid-close/reopen simulation) plus a routeWebSocket-mocked HALF-OPEN socket that stops answering pings without closing — and asserts a reconnect happens with NO focus/visibility/online event dispatched. Verified LOAD-BEARING by disabling the wake detector, rebuilding the client and re-running: FAILED with 'Expected: > 1, Received: 1' (the frozen-terminal defect). Fix restored, re-run green. Prior-iterate spec 77 fails environmentally here (WS upgrade succeeded → no outage), independent of this change.
